original_abstract: Precise control over the spatial distribution of chemical functionality within two-dimensional (2D) nanostructures remains a significant challenge in materials science. While living crystallization-driven self-assembly (CDSA) has achieved success in producing uniform 1D and 2D micelles, the epitaxial growth process typically results in compositionally homogeneous cores, limiting the potential for sophisticated, multi-responsive architectures. Here, we report a highly versatile seeded growth strategy using homopolymer/block copolymer blends of various crystallizable polylactones to fabricate 2D segmented platelet micelles with distinct, concentric core domains. By leveraging the compatible crystal lattices of different polyesters, we demonstrate the formation of complex multiblock architectures, including diblock, triblock, and hexablock co-micelles, with dimensions and compositions that can be precisely tuned. The structural integrity and spatial organization of these segmented domains were confirmed through atomic force microscopy (AFM) height profiling and stimulated emission depletion (STED) microscopy. Furthermore, we show that the inherent differences in hydrophobicity and chemical structure between the polylactone segments allow for programmed, selective hydrolytic degradation of specific core regions. This ability to spatially control both the internal chemistry and the degradation profile of 2D nanoparticles offers a robust platform for developing advanced stimuli-responsive materials for applications in compartmentalized cargo delivery and controlled-release systems.
humanized_abstract: Precise control over the spatial distribution of chemical functionality in two-dimensional (2D) nanostructures remains a major unsolved challenge in materials science. Although living crystallization-driven self-assembly (CDSA) has been demonstrated for the fabrication of uniform 1D and 2D micelles, the epitaxial growth process of CDSA generally affords compositionally homogeneous core regions, which precludes the realization of complex multi-responsive functional materials. Herein, we develop a highly versatile seeded growth approach by employing homopolymer/block copolymer blends of different crystallizable polylactones to fabricate 2D segmented platelet micelles with distinct, concentric core domains. Through the compatible crystal lattices of different polylactones, we demonstrate the complex multiblock structures of diblock, triblock and even hexablock co-micelles and achieve desired dimensions and compositions. The structures and spatial distributions of the segmented domains are clearly identified by both atomic force microscopy (AFM) height profiling and stimulated emission depletion (STED) microscopy. Besides, due to the differences in hydrophobicity and chemistry of polylactones, the complexes can realize programmed selective hydrolytic degradation of different core segments. Therefore, the 2D NP with controlled internal chemistry and degradation behaviors has shown great potential in the development of functional materials for compartmentalized cargo delivery and controlled release applications.
